name: Lahori Chargha
description: Whole chicken marinated in a bold spice rub and deep-fried until golden — a Lahore street-food icon.

name: Nihari
description: Slow-cooked beef shank stew with bone marrow, enriched with wheat and warm spices — eaten at dawn.

name: Murgh Cholay
description: Hearty chickpea and chicken curry with a tangy tomato base — classic Punjabi comfort food.

name: Lahori Fish Fry
description: River fish coated in a spiced gram-flour batter and fried crisp — a Lahore Food Street staple.

name: Sarson ka Saag
description: Slow-cooked mustard greens finished with butter, served with makki ki roti — winter soul food.

name: Aloo Gosht
description: Tender mutton and potatoes simmered in an onion-tomato masala — a Punjabi household staple.

name: Lahori Haleem
description: Slow-cooked blend of wheat, lentils, and shredded meat — rich, thick, and deeply spiced.

name: Tawa Chicken
description: Chicken pieces seared on a flat iron tawa with tomatoes, peppers, and tangy spices.

name: Karahi Gosht
description: Mutton cooked in a wok-style karahi with tomatoes, green chillies, and ginger — fragrant and fiery.

name: Daal Makhani
description: Black lentils simmered overnight with butter and cream — velvety, smoky, and indulgent.

name: Channay
description: Spiced chickpea curry tossed with onion and lemon, often served with fluffy bhaturay.

name: Kata Kat
description: Minced organ meats chopped on a hot tawa with spices — as bold as it sounds.

name: Seekh Kebab
description: Spiced minced beef skewered and grilled over charcoal — smoky and juicy.

name: Paye
description: Slow-cooked lamb trotters in a gelatinous, deeply spiced broth — a breakfast tradition.

name: Hareesa
description: Wheat and chicken porridge cooked for hours into a silky paste — Pakistani comfort in a bowl.

name: Lahori Biryani
description: Layered rice and spiced meat dum-cooked with saffron and fried onions — aromatic and festive.

name: Bun Kebab
description: Spiced lentil-and-meat patty in a bun with chutney and egg — Pakistan's original street burger.

name: Daal Chawal
description: Simple tempered lentils over steamed rice — everyday comfort food across Punjab.

name: Gulab Jamun
description: Deep-fried milk-solid dumplings soaked in rose-cardamom sugar syrup — melt-in-your-mouth bliss.

name: Jalebi
description: Crispy spirals of fermented batter dipped in saffron sugar syrup — crunchy and impossibly sweet.

name: Ras Malai
description: Soft paneer discs floating in chilled cardamom-saffron cream, topped with pistachios.

name: Gajar ka Halwa
description: Grated carrots slow-cooked in milk with sugar and ghee, garnished with almonds — winter royalty.

name: Kheer
description: Creamy rice pudding infused with cardamom and rose, garnished with silver leaf and almonds.

name: Sohan Halwa
description: Dense, translucent halwa from Multan studded with nuts — chewy and intensely sweet.

name: Barfi
description: Fudge-like milk sweet made with khoya and nuts, cut into diamond shapes — a festive essential.

name: Sindhi Biryani
description: Fiery, tangy mutton biryani loaded with potatoes, plums, and yoghurt — Sindh's pride.

name: Sai Bhaji
description: Mixed greens and lentil stew — nutritious, earthy, and quintessentially Sindhi.

name: Sindhi Kadhi
description: Tangy gram-flour curry with vegetables — sour, savory, and unique to Sindh.

name: Seyal Gosht
description: Mutton cooked in its own juices with onions and tomatoes — rustic Sindhi flavor.

name: Palla Fish
description: Indus River hilsa fish marinated and grilled — delicate, buttery, and prized.

name: Bhugal Gosht
description: Dry-roasted mutton with caramelized onions and bold spices — intense and smoky.

name: Sindhi Kachori
description: Crispy fried pastry filled with spiced lentils — a beloved Sindhi snack.

name: Achar Gosht
description: Meat cooked pickle-style with mustard oil and pickle spices — tangy and pungent.

name: Sindhi Tuk
description: Twice-fried potato slices with chilli-cumin seasoning — crispy, fluffy inside.

name: Sindhi Dampukht
description: Whole cuts of meat slow-cooked in a sealed pot with minimal spices — tender and aromatic.

name: Sindhi Daal
description: Tangy tempered red lentils — light, everyday Sindhi staple.

name: Thari Pulao
description: Desert-style aromatic rice pilaf from Tharparkar — simple, fragrant, and comforting.

name: Seyal Mahi
description: Fish simmered in a tomato-onion masala — Sindh's riverside classic.

name: Bhugal Murgh
description: Dry stir-fried chicken with whole spices and green chillies — bold and smoky.

name: Lolo ki Roti
description: Thick Sindhi millet flatbread baked in a tandoor — nutty, rustic, and filling.

name: Sindhi Kofta Curry
description: Spiced meatballs simmered in a rich onion-tomato gravy — hearty and satisfying.

name: Sindhi Pakora
description: Crunchy gram-flour fritters with onion and spices — perfect rainy-day snack.

name: Sindhi Halwa
description: Rich semolina halwa laden with ghee and nuts — Sindh's celebratory sweet.

name: Tosha
description: Layered flaky pastry sweetened with sugar — a traditional Sindhi delicacy.

name: Mal Pura
description: Sweet fried pancakes soaked in sugar syrup — golden, syrupy, and rich.

name: Pheni
description: Hair-thin vermicelli nests fried crispy and dipped in syrup — a delicate Sindhi treat.

name: Sindhi Mithai Thadal
description: Chilled almond-cardamom milk drink — refreshing and festive.

name: Lachha Mithai
description: Milk-based layered sweet with saffron threads — silky and aromatic.

name: Sindhi Ladoo
description: Round gram-flour sweets bound with ghee and sugar — nutty and crumbly.

name: Doodh Patti Chai Kulfi
description: Frozen milk tea kulfi — creamy chai flavor in a frosty treat.

name: Chapli Kebab
description: Flat spiced meat patties from Peshawar with pomegranate seeds and tomato — iconic Pashtun fare.

name: Namkeen Gosht
description: Salt and fat-roasted mutton — minimalist Pashtun cooking at its purest.

name: Peshawari Karahi
description: Mutton wok-fried with tomatoes and green chillies in pure animal fat — Peshawar's pride.

name: Kabuli Pulao
description: Fragrant rice pilaf with carrots, raisins, and lamb — a wedding-feast classic.

name: Charsi Tikka
description: Intensely spiced mutton chunks grilled over coal — addictively fiery, hence the name.

name: Peshawari Chapli Naan
description: Massive tandoori flatbread brushed with ghee — the perfect vessel for kebabs.

name: Rosh
description: Bone-in mutton slow-simmered in a spice-laden broth — soul-warming winter fare.

name: Sajji
description: Whole lamb leg skewered and roasted over open flame — smoky, juicy perfection.

name: Shinwari Karahi
description: Fatty mutton cooked in its own tallow with salt and tomato — rugged tribal flavor.

name: Mantu
description: Steamed meat dumplings topped with lentil sauce and yoghurt — Afghan-Pashtun fusion.

name: Seekh Kebab Peshawari
description: Charcoal-grilled minced meat skewers with cumin and coriander — smoky street staple.

name: Peshawari Chana
description: Spiced chickpeas with green chillies and lemon — a hearty vegetarian option.

name: Kabab Roll
description: Seekh kebab wrapped in naan with chutney and onion — Peshawar's grab-and-go meal.

name: Lamb Rosh Dumba
description: Fat-tailed lamb cooked in its own rendered fat — rich, primal, and traditional.

name: Swati Chicken Curry
description: Simple mountain chicken curry from Swat valley — light broth, big flavor.

name: Peshawari Daal
description: Creamy yellow lentils with cumin tempering — a daily KPK staple.

name: Peshawari Ice Cream
description: Dense, chewy ice cream loaded with pistachios and cream — thick enough to slice.

name: Sheer Khurma
description: Vermicelli pudding in sweetened milk with dates and pistachios — an Eid staple.

name: Gur Cake
description: Dense jaggery cake baked in village ovens — earthy brown-sugar warmth.

name: Kahwa
description: Green tea brewed with cinnamon, cardamom, and crushed almonds — a Pashtun welcome ritual.

name: Peshawari Falooda
description: Layered rose syrup, vermicelli, and ice cream sundae — Peshawar's coolest indulgence.

name: Peshawari Gulabi Chai
description: Frothy pink noon chai with cardamom and cream — Pashtun tea-time elegance.

name: Jaggery Walnut Barfi
description: Walnut fudge sweetened with gur — rustic mountain confection.

name: Malida
description: Crumbled roti mixed with ghee and sugar — simple Pashtun sweet for celebrations.

name: Peshawari Laddu
description: Gram-flour balls with pistachios and ghee — golden, nutty, and festive.

name: Balochi Sajji
description: Whole lamb stuffed with rice and roasted over open coals — Baluchistan's crown jewel.

name: Kaak
description: Hard, ring-shaped bread designed for nomadic travel — crunchy and long-lasting.

name: Dampukht
description: Sealed-pot mutton slow-cooked in bone broth — simple seasonings, maximum tenderness.

name: Balochi Kebab
description: Hand-minced lamb kebabs grilled over juniper embers — wild and smoky.

name: Landi
description: Sun-dried and smoked mutton — Baluchi preservation method turned delicacy.

name: Balochi Pulao
description: Aromatic rice pilaf with lamb chunks and whole spices — festive Baluchi comfort.

name: Khaddi Kebab
description: Whole lamb roasted underground in a pit — smoky, tender, and celebratory.

name: Balochi Siri Paye
description: Lamb head and trotters slow-simmered overnight — gelatinous and nourishing breakfast.

name: Dum Pukht Mutton
description: Dough-sealed clay pot mutton — fall-off-the-bone tender with concentrated juices.

name: Balochi Tikka
description: Cubes of salted mutton grilled over hot coals — pure meat, pure fire.

name: Makrani Fish Curry
description: Coastal Makran fish simmered in tomato-chilli gravy — sea meets spice.

name: Tabahdegh
description: Rice with crispy bottom crust and tender lamb — the Baluchi tahdig.

name: Daal Gosht Balochi
description: Lentils and mutton cooked together in a hearty stew — Baluchi daily fuel.

name: Balochi Aloo Gosht
description: Potato and mutton curry with cumin and black pepper — simple and filling.

name: Makrani Prawn Curry
description: Jumbo prawns in a fiery coastal curry — Gwadar's gift to foodies.

name: Balochi Daal
description: Slow-cooked mixed lentils with cumin and turmeric — hearty desert staple.

name: Tanoor Roti
description: Giant clay-oven bread slapped on tanoor walls — smoky, chewy, and rustic.

name: Kachri Gosht
description: Mutton tenderized with dried wild melon (kachri) — unique desert flavor.

name: Balochi Halwa
description: Dense wheat halwa drenched in ghee and studded with almonds — serious sweetness.

name: Shillanchi
description: Sweet milk dessert thickened with rice flour — Balochi comfort in a bowl.

name: Makrani Dates Halwa
description: Dates pounded with ghee and almonds into a rich halwa — nature's candy, elevated.

name: Balochi Sharbat
description: Cool rose-and-lemon drink for the desert heat — refreshing and aromatic.

name: Gur Wala Doodh
description: Warm milk sweetened with jaggery — Baluchi bedtime ritual.

name: Balochi Kulfi
description: Dense frozen milk dessert with pistachios — slow-churned and incredibly creamy.

name: Khajoor Barfi
description: Date and nut fudge squares rolled in coconut — natural sweetness, no refined sugar.
